perturbation.original_text: In the event of a culpable breach, the Partner shall be liable to M&R for compensation for the damage caused and shall keep M&R indemnified from all resulting claims and costs. If it is certain that confidential information found its way to third parties from the sphere of the Partner, a culpable (at least negligent) breach of non- disclosure obligations shall be assumed - except when there is evidence to t he contrary.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of a culpable breach, the Partner shall be liable to M&R for compensation for the damage caused and shall keep M&R indemnified from all resulting claims and costs. If it is certain that confidential information found its way to third parties from the sphere of the Partner, a culpable (at least negligent) breach of non- disclosure obligations shall be assumed - except when there is evidence to t he contrary. M&R accepts no liability for the use or the t rust in the correctness or completeness of the confidential information disclosed to the Partner.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'No Liability' clause into the 'Consequences of Breach' section implies that M&R's lack of liability extends even when the partner breaches the agreement, obscuring the partner's responsibility in such cases.
perturbation.law_explanation: ABGB §1295 establishes a general right to damages for culpable harm. By implying M&R bears no liability even in case of a breach by the partner, the contract obscures the partner's potential responsibility for damages resulting from their own breach, thereby contradicting the fundamental principle of liability for causing harm.

perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a 'No Liability' clause for M&R, even when the partner breaches the agreement. This obscures the partner's responsibility in such cases. The Law Explanation indicates that ABGB §1295 establishes a general right to damages for culpable harm, implying liability for causing harm. Scraped Law Snippet 2 further supports this by stating that 'Als Schadenersatz bezeichnet man den Ausgleich oder die Wiedergutmachung eines Schadens, der einer Person entstanden ist' (Compensation refers to the compensation or redress of damage caused to a person). The introduction of a blanket 'no liability' clause for M&R weakens the partner's right to claim damages for a breach, which is a fundamental principle, thus creating a contradiction with the law.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement is governed by Austrian law excluding its conflict of law rules. The competent court of jurisdiction for all disputes arising from this agreement shall be Graz.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement is governed by Austrian law excluding its conflict of law rules. The competent court of jurisdiction for all disputes arising from this agreement shall be Grambach, date ........................... XXX, date ................................................ ……………………………………. ………………………………………………. M&R Automation GmbH Partner
perturbation.explanation: Moving the signature blocks to the jurisdiction clause confuses the intention of jurisdiction and reduces enforceability, as the signatures are meant for agreement of terms and not to define court of jurisdiction.
perturbation.law_explanation: ZPO §27 specifies the requirements for establishing jurisdiction. Inserting signature blocks muddles the clear declaration of the competent court, creating ambiguity about the intended legal venue and reduces its enforceability.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 27 ZPO - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Zivilprozessordnung § 27 Besonderer Gerichtsstand der Erbschaft (1) Klagen, welche die Feststellung des Erbrechts, Ansprüche des Erben gegen einen Erbschaftsbesitzer, Ansprüche aus Vermächtnissen oder sonstigen Verfügungen von Todes wegen, Pflichtteilsansprüche oder die Teilung der Erbschaft zum Gegenstand haben, können vor dem Gericht erhoben werden, bei dem der Erblasser zur Zeit seines Todes den allgemeinen Gerichtsstand gehabt hat. (2) Ist der Erblasser ein Deutscher und hatte er zur Zeit seines Todes im Inland keinen allgemeinen Gerichtsstand, so können die im Absatz 1 bezeichneten Klagen vor dem Gericht erhoben werden, in dessen Bezirk der Erblasser seinen letzten inländischen Wohnsitz hatte; wenn er einen solchen Wohnsitz nicht hatte, so gilt die Vorschrift des § 15 Abs. 1 Satz 2 entsprechend. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

(2) Ist der Erblasser ein Deutscher und hatte er zur Zeit seines Todes im Inland keinen allgemeinen Gerichtsstand, so können die im Absatz 1 bezeichneten Klagen vor dem Gericht erhoben wer
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly specifies Graz as the competent court of jurisdiction. The changed text introduces extraneous information—signature blocks and a date—into the jurisdiction clause, potentially confusing the clear declaration of the competent court. This confusion, as the Law Explanation states, creates ambiguity about the intended legal venue, undermining its enforceability. ZPO §27, as provided in both scraped snippets, relates to jurisdiction but specifically for inheritance matters, which is irrelevant here. However, the key issue is the structural flaw, as the Perturbation Explanation states, that moving signature blocks into the jurisdiction clause introduces ambiguity and reduces enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination of this agreement, all the confidential information made available by M&R shall be returned to M&R on request of M&R, all copies that were made shall be destroyed or handed over to M&R and all stored information shall be deleted.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination of this agreement, all the confidential information made available by M&R shall be returned to M&R on request of M&R, all copies that were made shall be destroyed or handed over to M&R and all stored information shall be deleted. This agreement is governed by Austrian law excluding its conflict of law rules.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the governing law provision to the termination clause makes it ambiguous whether Austrian law only applies upon termination, potentially leading to misinterpretations regarding the contract's overall legal framework.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Austrian Federal Act on Private International Law (IPR-Gesetz) §1
perturbation.law_explanation: IPR-Gesetz §1 dictates how to determine applicable law. By placing the governing law provision within the termination clause, the agreement creates uncertainty if Austrian law is intended to govern the entire agreement from its inception, or only upon termination. This undermines the clarity required by private international law for determining the applicable legal framework.
perturbation.justification: The placement of the governing law provision solely within the termination clause creates a structural flaw that introduces ambiguity regarding the contract's overall legal framework. This ambiguity contradicts the clarity required for determining the applicable legal framework, as suggested by the Law Explanation, and weakens the contract's enforceability from inception. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction' definition, where a required legal element (governing law) is placed in a misleading section, affecting its interpretation.
